pi_items.source: https://epsg.io/4326 and https://spatialreference.org/ref/epsg/4326/ - WGS84 geographic coordinate system confirmed
https://epsg.io/2227 - NAD83 / California zone 3 (ftUS) confirmed with Lambert Conformal Conic projection
https://latitude.to/articles-by-country/us/united-states/3316/mission-san-francisco-de-asis - Official Mission Dolores (Mission San Francisco de Asís) coordinates at 16th and Dolores Street

https://www.usgs.gov/faqs/how-much-distance-does-a-degree-minute-and-second-cover-your-maps - 0.001 degrees equals approximately 111 meters (111.32 m), which is reasonable for landmark location tolerance

pi_items.metadata_value: Real SF landmark coordinates used to create synthetic shapefile
pi_items.verified_value: setup_task.sh hardcodes 4 landmark coordinates and uses ogr2ogr to convert from GeoJSON to EPSG:2227 shapefile, then deletes .prj file
pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh lines 20-40: coordinates are hardcoded from real SF landmarks

pi_items.verified_value: EPSG:2227 covers: Alameda, Calaveras, Contra Costa, Madera, Marin, Mariposa, Merced, Mono, San Francisco, San Joaquin, San Mateo, Santa Clara, Santa Cruz, Stanislaus, and Tuolumne counties

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real San Francisco landmark coordinates for Golden Gate Bridge South (37.8199°N, -122.4783°W), Coit Tower (37.8024°N, -122.4058°W), and Palace of Fine Arts (37.8029°N, -122.4484°W) with accuracy within 6, 3, and 20 meters respectively. However, the Mission Dolores coordinates in metadata (37.7641°N, -122.4269°W) are contradicted by official sources which place Mission Dolores at 37.7586°N, -122.4229°W (approximately 757 meters difference). The task correctly specifies EPSG:4326 for WGS84 target CRS and EPSG:2227 for NAD83/California Zone 3 input CRS. The tolerance of 0.001 degrees equals approximately 111 meters. The data is synthetic, generated from hardcoded landmark coordinates via ogr2ogr in setup_task.sh.
